concept: Private / Under Chosen Limits of Access or Attention
description: Kept from uninvited access or attention by a person or group choosing that boundary. The adjective applies to a place, conversation, record, or stretch of time even when nobody has hidden it. 'kupe' (hide) concerns concealment; 'mirewu' concerns who may enter, observe, or know.
sound_symbolism: Personal 'mi' begins at the speaker's own center. 'Re' turns attention back at the boundary, while rounded 'wu' leaves quiet room inside it.
grammatical_notes: A predicative or attributive adjective: 'ha lokue mirewu nai.' (This place is private.) The quality-noun rule supplies privacy. Unlike 'kupela' (secret) or 'sonu' (alone), it describes a limit on access rather than hidden information or the absence of company.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Privacy leaves room for rest and reflection without treating solitude as disappearance.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: With 'mirewu', a closed door or withheld record becomes a stated limit rather than a rebuke.

tags.cognition: chosen limits on access to attention or information
tags.community: a personal or shared boundary respected by others
tags.spatial: a place reserved from uninvited entry or observation
